name: ossuary
summary: A chamber of ordered bones — walls lined with skulls, columns of femurs, ornamental patterns made from the dead. The faithful kept for veneration, not feared.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: rectangular or cruciform — processional paths between bone stacks

primary_palette.note: a memorial altar with pillars of skulls to each side
the founder's sarcophagus amid the piled dead
the chamber's ornament is the ceiling itself

story_prompts: newly arranged — a recent plague's dead still being sorted into patterns
venerated daily — candles lit, fresh petals among the skulls
desecrated — bones scattered, patterns broken, an empty niche where a reliquary sat
a grief-stricken visitor — found weeping at a particular skull set apart from the others
a secret below — one sarcophagus has been dragged aside, a crawl-space behind it

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: low and cold (0.15-0.25) — scattered candles throwing long shadows through bone arches

anti_patterns: bones are arranged, never scattered — this is veneration, not a battlefield
processional paths between stacks; visitors can walk the chamber
no throne or ritual circle — ossuaries memorialize, they do not perform
